channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: WNBA Board Approves Sale of Connecticut Sun to Tilman Fertitta; Team Will Relocate to Houston After 2026 Season
item.description: The WNBA and NBA boards of governors have unanimously approved the sale of the Connecticut Sun from the Mohegan Tribe to Houston Rockets owner Tilman J. Fertitta. The team will remain in Connecticut f...

item.title: Early 20th Century French Postcards Depicting Young Women Celebrating April Fool’s Day
item.description: This article explores the history and imagery of early 20th-century French postcards featuring young women celebrating April Fool’s Day, known in French as 'poisson d’avril.' The tradition of April Fo...

item.title: IMF Warns Pakistan of Economic Vulnerability Due to Gulf Dependence
item.description: Following the release of a $1.1 billion tranche for Pakistan, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) has flagged the country’s heavy reliance on the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) as the most pressing ...

item.title: Unable to Access Preston Weather Report Due to Site Restrictions
item.description: The page intended to provide a weather update for Preston cannot be accessed due to News Corp Australia's web security measures. Visitors may encounter a message indicating they have been detected as ...

item.title: USS Alaska’s Visit to Gibraltar Highlights U.S. Nuclear Deterrence Amid Iran Tensions
item.description: The U.S. Navy’s Ohio-class ballistic missile submarine, the USS Alaska, made a rare public stop in Gibraltar, drawing international attention amid heightened tensions between the United States and Ira...

item.title: Rep. Jim Jordan challenged on past comments about gas prices amid rising costs
item.description: During a recent segment on CNN's 'The Source,' Representative Jim Jordan (R-OH), a leading candidate for Speaker of the U.S. House of Representatives, faced pointed questions from journalist Kaitlan C...

item.title: Gauteng Premier Highlights Education Reform as Pillar for South Africa's Future
item.description: Gauteng Premier Panyaza Lesufi has emphasised that improving education is central to building a stronger South Africa and a thriving economy. Speaking at the media launch of the Golden Jubilee commemo...

item.title: Florida Gubernatorial Candidate Loses Volunteer Following Offensive Online Comment
item.description: James Fishback, a far-right Florida gubernatorial candidate, lost a key campaign volunteer after allegedly sending her a message containing an obscure internet slur. Gianna Tagliarini, the Hillsboroug...

item.title: Five Italian Tourists Die in Maldives Cave Diving Accident
item.description: A diving expedition in the Maldives ended in tragedy after five Italian tourists, including a mother and daughter, were found dead inside an underwater cave in Vaavu Atoll, roughly 100 kilometres sout...

item.title: Bilawal Clarifies PPP Not Consulted on Constitutional Amendment Plans
item.description: ISLAMABAD – PPP Chairman Bilawal Bhutto-Zardari has stated that neither he nor President Asif Ali Zardari have been contacted by the government regarding any constitutional amendment, amid speculation...

item.title: Kuse aims to become Eastern Cape’s first WBC boxing champion
item.description: Siyakholwa “One Way” Kuse is set to challenge reigning WBC mini flyweight world champion Melvin “El Gringo” Jerusalem this Saturday at Emperors Palace in Johannesburg. Hailing from Mdantsane, the East...

item.title: Election Commission rolls out Phase III of voter list revision across 16 states and 3 UTs
item.description: The Election Commission of India (ECI) has instructed chief electoral officers of 16 states and three Union Territories to commence the third phase of the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral...

item.title: Ali MacGraw’s Career and Style Evolution in the 1980s
item.description: Ali MacGraw, born April 1, 1939, achieved early fame with her roles in 'Goodbye, Columbus' (1969) and 'Love Story' (1970), earning a Golden Globe and an Academy Award nomination. By 1972, she was reco...

item.title: Khana LGA Plans to Revive Sports in Primary and Secondary Schools
item.description: The chairman of Khana Local Government Area (LGA) in Rivers State, Bariere Thomas, don yan say dem dey plan to restart school sports for primary and secondary schools inside the area. E talk say schoo...

item.title: Madlanga Commission of Inquiry Resumes Proceedings in Pretoria
item.description: The Madlanga Commission of Inquiry, which is investigating allegations of criminal conduct, political interference, and corruption within South Africa's criminal justice system, continued its hearings...

item.title: SADC Engages at Africa Forward Summit 2026 to Boost Regional Development and Partnerships
item.description: The Southern African Development Community (SADC) was actively represented at the inaugural Africa Forward Summit 2026 held in Nairobi from 11th to 12th May, under the theme 'Africa-France Partnership...

item.title: Solo parent wins P35 million Lotto 6/42 jackpot in Negros Occidental
item.description: A solo parent from Negros Occidental experienced a life-changing stroke of luck when he won the P35 million jackpot in the Lotto 6/42 draw on May 2, 2026. The bettor had just finished buying items for...

item.title: Former Becerra Aide Pleads Guilty in Federal Fraud Case Amid Questions Over Campaign Funds
item.description: Dana Williamson, a longtime political operative with ties to Xavier Becerra, Gov. Gavin Newsom, and former Gov. Jerry Brown, pleaded guilty Thursday in federal court in Sacramento to charges related t...

item.title: Families of Drug War Victims Call for ICC Arrest of Bato Dela Rosa
item.description: Families of victims killed during the Duterte administration's anti-drug campaign have publicly called for accountability as Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa faces an International Criminal Court (ICC)...

item.title: Fairfax County Prosecutor Denies Leniency Toward Undocumented Immigrants
item.description: Stephen Descano, the attorney for Fairfax County, Virginia, strongly refuted claims made by U.S. House Judiciary Committee Chairman Jim Jordan (R-OH) that his office does not prosecute undocumented im...
